description: Import from actors.base_actor fails — actors/ package does not exist anywhere in the project. BaseActor only exists as a concept in .docs/usage_integration_examples/base_actor_communication.py
proposed_fix: Move BaseActor into src/actors/base_actor.py with __init__.py then change import to from src.actors.base_actor import BaseActor

description: Import from contracts.communication_gateway fails — contracts/ package does not exist. ICommunicationGateway is at src/wiring/contracts/communication_gateway.py
proposed_fix: Change import to from src.wiring.contracts.communication_gateway import ICommunicationGateway

description: Import from actors.base_actor fails — actors/ package does not exist. Same root cause as BC-TST-001
proposed_fix: Change import to from src.actors.base_actor import BaseActor after creating src/actors/ package

description: Import from contracts.communication_gateway fails — contracts/ package does not exist. Same root cause as BC-TST-002
proposed_fix: Change import to from src.wiring.contracts.communication_gateway import ICommunicationGateway

description: Duplicate MockGateway class (lines 21-65) with different interface (should_fail bool) vs conftest.py version (raise_on_send Exception). Modifying one breaks consistency with the other
proposed_fix: Remove MockGateway from test_actor_isolation.py and rely on the conftest.py version; update should_fail to raise_on_send in tests that use it

description: Test file test_live_protocol_switch.py and .docs/usage_integration_examples/tests/test_fabric_integration.py test the exact same scenario: live protocol switch + readiness stability + session cleanup. Dual maintenance burden
proposed_fix: Merge into test_fabric_integration.py and delete test_live_protocol_switch.py or vice versa

description: Shell script uses #!/usr/bin/env bash jq python3 and Unix paths — will not run on Windows. Not cross-platform; assumes bash and jq are installed
proposed_fix: Convert to pytest-based test or provide a PowerShell .ps1 equivalent for Windows compatibility

description: event_loop fixture defined in both conftest.py (line 85) and test_fabric_integration.py (line 22) with identical session-scoped implementation. Causes pytest fixture redefinition warning and potential override confusion
proposed_fix: Remove one of the duplicate event_loop fixtures; keep in conftest.py for global reuse
